authoritative_sources.title: About Us
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAOB is an official IATF oversight office and publicly states that it oversees consistent application of IATF 16949 to improve quality and performance in the automotive supply chain.
used_for: boundary framing for compliance support versus formal system authority

authoritative_sources.title: Performance Complaints
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAOB's performance-complaint workflow is an authoritative public reference for when chronic quality, delivery, or process failures under an IATF-governed quality system must escalate beyond ordinary plant handling.
used_for: escalation thresholds for recurring quality or delivery breakdowns

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 565 - Vehicle Identification Number Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 565 is the current federal rule for VIN format, uniqueness, check digit, and plant-of-manufacture coding, making it authoritative for vehicle identity and traceability grounding.

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 573 - Defect and Noncompliance Responsibility and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 573 is the current federal rule for safety-related defect and noncompliance reporting, making it authoritative for when product-safety concerns must escalate beyond ordinary internal compliance review.

authoritative_sources.title: Resources Related to Investigations and Recalls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. vehicle-safety regulator, and this public page is authoritative for VIN recall verification, recall workflow resources, and complaint or manufacturer-communication routing that change release posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Ford Motor Company Customer-Specific Requirements for IATF 16949:2016
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Ford's public customer-specific requirements are authoritative for organizations manufacturing automotive production and service parts and explicitly cover MMOG/LE, part identification and tracking, nonconforming product, customer notification, release, and warranty-related expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control applicability and customer-specific obligation mapping

authoritative_sources.title: IATF 16949 - GM Customer-Specific Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GM's public customer-specific requirements are authoritative for organizations manufacturing automotive production and service parts and define supplier-change, deviation, containment, and customer-approval boundaries that this lane cannot overrule.

authoritative_sources.title: (MMOG/LE) Global Materials Management Operations Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is the recognized automotive industry standards body. Its MMOG/LE reference is authoritative for materials-management capability across inventory, delivery, supplier coordination, and service-part flow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory, supplier, and materials-management evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: AIAG Traceability Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's traceability guideline is a recognized public automotive reference for product visibility, recall-scope reduction, and traceability fire-drill expectations across the supply chain.

workflow_stages.name: request intake and automotive-scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify plant, program, customer, product family, serial or VIN scope, jurisdiction, supplier context, and requested decision before any compliance recommendation is prepared.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map customer-specific requirements, traceability, product-safety, nonconformance, containment, hold, release, and change-control obligations to the exact records and owners needed for the work item.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and review readiness
workflow_stages.description: Collect and compare production, serial or lot, inventory, supplier, containment, nonconformance, and release artifacts needed to support a bounded compliance conclusion.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability, supplier, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible genealogy, inventory, supplier-change evidence, hold state, and release prerequisites support traceable handling or require blocked escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, remediation, and audit preparation
workflow_stages.description: Translate missing evidence, policy-versus-practice drift, supplier issues, safety implications, hold conflicts, and audit questions into remediation and review-ready packets without claiming final signoff authority.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, build history, serial or VIN genealogy, lot or date-code records, and shipment history
inventory states, warehouse movements, suspect-stock or hold records, and release records
control plans, reaction plans, layered audit findings, product-safety escalation records, traceability fire-drill evidence, and internal audit findings
nonconformance reports, containment records, controlled-shipping records, deviation or concession requests, and change-control packages
supplier approval status, incoming inspection records, material certifications, PPAP or change submissions, and supplier corrective-action responses
customer-notification packages, audit-preparation notes, evidence checklists, and owner-routed remediation summaries
systems_of_record: customer portal or OEM requirements repository

allowed: classify automotive compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, evidence-gap summaries, audit-ready packets, and remediation escalations tied to named artifacts and owners
determine whether visible control obligations and evidence are satisfied, missing, stale, or in conflict without improvising final release, supplier approval, or legal authority
escalate: required tenant facts, jurisdiction context, release ownership, or source-of-truth mappings are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, customer, or tenant-policy conflicts materially change traceability, hold, release, supplier, safety, or recall posture
traceability, hold, supplier-change, controlled-shipping, or nonconformance evidence gaps increase release, safety, or customer-risk exposure
requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal signoff, final release authority, or supplier approval
refuse: requests for final legal signoff, guaranteed regulator acceptance, or final release approval
requests to proceed with shipment, substitution, concession, or rework when required serial, VIN, lot, supplier, hold, or containment evidence is missing or contradictory
requests that collapse the lane into generic compliance administration and ignore automotive traceability, supplier, or release realities

domain_failure_modes: Allowing shipment or release to proceed even though traceability, hold, supplier-change, or containment evidence is missing or contradictory.
Treating serial, VIN, or lot-record corrections as clerical cleanup instead of a controlled compliance event that affects containment and downstream scope.
Applying a tenant SOP or customer instruction that conflicts with the public regulatory or customer-specific baseline without escalating the conflict.
Treating supplier substitutions or material-certification mismatches as routine sourcing issues even though supplier approval and release boundaries are unresolved.
Producing a confident compliance conclusion without named systems of record, accountable evidence owners, or current release authority context.
Collapsing automotive compliance into legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, product-safety signoff, or final quality disposition that belongs to adjacent owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff

notes: The public baseline is anchored on U.S. vehicle-safety regulation, IAOB oversight, public OEM customer-specific requirements, and AIAG materials-management and traceability references. Customer-specific portals, customer contracts, export controls, and plant release matrices must be supplied through tenant retrieval when they materially change the answer.

The specialist is intentionally structured as automotive compliance support labor and must not imply final legal, regulator-facing, engineering, supplier-approval, product-safety, or quality-release authority.
